newsSince: ChipMOS reported May 2026 revenue of NT$2,384.3m (US$76.0m), +17.7% YoY though -3.1% MoM, citing a persistent AI-related memory demand/supply imbalance and ongoing footprint expansion under long-term supply agreements. Constructive for backend memory packaging/test, but not CPO-specific.

verdicts.rankRationale: Through a co-packaged-optics lens, ChipMOS has near-zero CPO exposure - it is memory/display OSAT. The +17.7% May revenue print is AI-memory driven, not optics-relevant, and the thesis already flags low CPO exposure. No CPO catalyst since June 9. Hold near the bottom.
verdicts.bull: AI memory demand/supply imbalance lifts backend volumes; long-term supply agreements and capacity adds support throughput.
verdicts.neutral: Healthy memory cycle, but irrelevant to the CPO thesis this page screens for.
verdicts.bear: No co-packaged-optics content; rank correctly sits at the page floor.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Memory/display cycle rollover; remains a non-CPO name

verdicts.rankRationale: Scaling lens rewards backend packaging/test capacity feeding AI compute. May revenue +17.7% YoY on an AI-memory imbalance plus footprint expansion under long-term agreements modestly confirms the test-time-scaling backend thesis. Small positive, but a routine monthly print - hold rank, +1 score.
verdicts.bull: AI-memory packaging/test demand and capacity expansion give direct backend exposure to the scaling buildout; +17.7% YoY revenue.
verdicts.neutral: Monthly revenue solid but MoM softened -3.1%; OSAT margins remain cyclical.
verdicts.bear: Commodity OSAT with memory-cycle beta; +115% YTD already prices much of the recovery.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued AI-memory packaging volume / capacity ramp
